steps.title: Focus on age similarity
steps.content: The student's goal is to emphasize a similarity between the ages of Qianmen Dajie and Rue Mouffetard. The relevant notes say Qianmen Dajie has existed since the Ming dynasty and Rue Mouffetard since the mid-Roman Empire, so both malls are very old.

steps.title: Compare the first three choices
steps.content: A says Qianmen Dajie has roots as far back as the Ming dynasty and, likewise, Rue Mouffetard has existed for centuries, which compares the two malls by age. B names Qianmen Dajie in Beijing and Rue Mouffetard in Paris, which emphasizes place and fame. C defines pedestrian malls as outdoor streets closed to vehicle traffic, which does not address age.

steps.title: Reject the unrelated option
steps.content: D says the two places are examples of pedestrian malls and mentions that pedestrian malls proliferated in Europe in the 19th and 20th centuries. It does not emphasize the ages of these two specific malls. The choice built around their long histories best accomplishes the student's goal. The answer is A.
